accessibility.simpleModeDescription: Reduce visual detail for easier scanning.
accessibility.pageDescription: How Health Made Clear supports accessible learning.
accessibility.commitmentBody: We aim for WCAG 2.1 Level AA where possible. Health information should be readable, navigable by keyboard, and adaptable to your needs.
accessibility.featuresList: Adjustable text size, light and dark themes, simple mode (reduced visual effects), skip to main content, keyboard-friendly navigation, and printable tools.
accessibility.limitsBody: Some decorative imagery may lack detailed descriptions. We are improving glossary deep links and locale-specific emergency numbers.
accessibility.contactBody: If you have trouble using this site, contact us through the About page.

disclaimer.educational: This content is for educational purposes only. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
disclaimer.educationalLong: The content on Health Made Clear is designed for educational purposes to help you better understand health concepts. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
disclaimer.emergencyBody: Do not rely on this platform for urgent medical needs. Call your local emergency number immediately.
disclaimer.emergencyCallAria: Call emergency services (911 in the US)

common.noResultsTryAnother: No glossary terms match your search. Try another word.
common.noLessonsTryBroader: No lessons match your search yet. Try a broader term.

glossary.title: Health Glossary
glossary.description: Clear, everyday definitions for medical terms so you can understand your health journey without the confusion.

learn.title: What would you like to learn today?
learn.description: Clear, reliable guides to help you navigate your health journey with confidence.
learn.notFoundBody: The lesson you are looking for is not available right now.
learn.sidebarBody: Bring these questions to your next visit or pharmacy stop.
learn.tipMissedDose: Ask what happens if you miss a dose.
learn.pharmacistTip: Pharmacists can explain labels in simple language.
learn.sidebarBodyDoctor: Prepare for your next doctor's visit.
learn.tipDoctorAppointmentTime: Write down the date and time.
learn.tipDoctorInsurance: Bring your insurance card and photo ID.
learn.tipDoctorQuestions: Make a list of questions to ask.
learn.doctorTip: Your doctor is there to partner with you on your health.
learn.tipLabsFasting: Check if you need to fast.
learn.tipLabsHydration: Drink plenty of water before the test.
learn.tipLabsComfort: Wear comfortable clothing with loose sleeves.
learn.tipLabsResults: Ask when and how you will get your results.
learn.labsTip: Lab technicians can explain the testing process.
learn.quizSubtitle: Take a short quiz to check your understanding of this lesson.

home.introTitle: Start with a short guided introduction.
home.introBody: We designed a calm, beginner-friendly set of lessons to help you understand health terms and prepare for appointments.
home.featuredPathsBody: Curated journeys to help you understand specific health topics.
home.whyMattersBody: 9 out of 10 adults struggle to understand basic health information. Clearer education helps people prepare for visits, ask better questions, and follow care plans more confidently.
home.valueKnowledgeBody: Understanding medical terms and treatment options helps you make informed decisions about your care.
home.valueConfidenceBody: When you understand what your doctor is saying, you can ask the right questions and get the most out of every appointment.
home.valueAccessBody: Health education should not cost money or require a medical degree. All our content is free and written in plain language.
home.toolsBody: Explore lessons, look up medical terms in plain language, or use practical tools for your next visit.
home.learnPreviewBody: Browse our library of clear lessons on common health topics, from medication safety to preventive care.
home.glossaryPreviewBody: Look up any medical term and get a simple, clear definition you can understand.
home.toolsPreviewBody: Printable checklists, visit planners, and care guides to help you navigate healthcare with confidence.

meta.siteTitle: Health Made Clear - Understand Your Health
meta.siteDescription: Free, accessible health education for everyone. Learn about health topics in plain language.

hero.title: Understand Your Health, One Clear Step at a Time
hero.subtitle: Free, easy-to-understand health information to help you prepare for visits, understand medical language, and feel more confident making health decisions.
hero.preparedVisitsBody: Arrive with questions and notes ready.
hero.imageAlt: Healthcare professional consulting with a patient in a calm clinical setting

about.title: Our mission is clarity.
about.description: We believe understanding your health should not require a medical degree. Health Made Clear is dedicated to translating complex medical language into plain, actionable language so more people can make informed decisions.
about.valueEducationBody: The content in Health Made Clear is strictly educational and informational. It is designed to support conversations with healthcare professionals, not replace them.
about.valueEveryoneBody: From high-contrast colors to readable typography and printable tools, every part of the platform is designed to be easier to use.
about.valueStructuredBody: Information is organized into guided paths and short lessons so people can build confidence without overload.
about.warningBody: Never disregard professional medical advice or delay seeking it because of something you read here. If you think you may have a medical emergency, call your doctor or emergency services immediately.
about.contactBody: Questions or feedback? Reach us at
about.whyMattersBody: Low health literacy is linked to poorer outcomes, higher healthcare costs, and more frequent hospitalizations. Clearer education can help people prepare, ask better questions, and follow care plans more confidently.
about.educationDetail: The content provided on Health Made Clear is for educational purposes only. It is meant to make health information easier to understand and support patient confidence.
about.joinBody: Improving health literacy is a community effort. Whether you are a clinician reviewing content for accuracy, a designer improving usability, or a patient sharing lived experience, your contribution matters.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: How we handle your information at Health Made Clear.
privacy.educationBody: Health Made Clear is an educational resource. We do not collect sensitive personal health information or provide medical diagnoses or treatments.
privacy.collectBody: We only store data locally on your device to remember your learning progress, language, theme, and text size preferences. This data is never transmitted to our servers.
privacy.controlBody: You can clear your browser storage at any time to reset your preferences and progress.

tools.pageDescription: Navigating healthcare does not have to be confusing. Use these practical tools to prepare for visits, understand care options, and ask better questions.
tools.askDescription: Generate a personalized list of questions to bring to your next doctor's appointment.
tools.careDescription: Compare home care, primary care, urgent care, and emergency care in clear language.
tools.labelsDescription: Open our lesson on reading prescriptions, dosage, warnings, and refill details.
tools.checklistDescription: Use a printable checklist so you have your ID, insurance, records, and notes ready.
tools.plannerDescription: Prepare for your upcoming appointment by building a custom list of questions and notes so nothing gets missed.
tools.selectQuestionsBody: Choose the questions that matter most for your upcoming visit.
tools.addNotes: Add your own questions or notes
tools.notesPlaceholder: For example: I also noticed a slight pain when I walk upstairs.

new-symptom: What could be causing this symptom?
When should I expect to feel better?
medication: Why am I taking this medicine?
What side effects should I watch for?
What should I do if I miss a dose?
Can I take this with my other medicines?
followup: Is my treatment working as expected?
Do I need any changes to my plan?
What results should we review today?
When should I follow up again?

tools.checklistPageDescription: Use this printable checklist to make sure you have the information and items you need before leaving for your appointment.
checklistItems: Recent test results or discharge papers
A notebook or phone for notes
Water and any comfort items you need
tools.careGuideTitle: Where should I go for care?
tools.careGuideDescription: Choosing the right place for care can save time, money, and stress. Use this guide to compare common options.
tools.homeCareBody: Rest, fluids, and over-the-counter medicine for mild symptoms that improve within a few days.
tools.primaryCareBody: Your regular doctor for checkups, ongoing conditions, and non-urgent new symptoms.
tools.urgentCareBody: Walk-in clinics for problems that need same-day attention but are not life-threatening.
tools.emergencyBody: Severe chest pain, trouble breathing, stroke signs, heavy bleeding, or other life-threatening symptoms.
tools.homeCareChecklist: Mild cold symptoms|Low fever|Minor cuts or scrapes|Muscle aches from exercise
tools.primaryCareChecklist: Annual checkups|Ongoing conditions|New symptoms lasting several days|Prescription refills
tools.urgentCareChecklist: Sprains or minor fractures|Stitches for small cuts|Moderate fever|Urinary tract infection symptoms
tools.emergencyChecklist: Chest pain or pressure|Trouble breathing|Stroke signs (FAST)|Severe bleeding or head injury
tools.scenarioSoreThroatBody: No trouble breathing, can drink fluids, mild fever — start with home care or call primary care if not improving.
tools.scenarioChestPainTitle: Chest pain with shortness of breath
tools.scenarioChestPainBody: Sudden chest pressure, pain spreading to arm or jaw, or trouble breathing — call 911 immediately.
tools.whenInDoubtBody: If you are unsure how serious symptoms are, call your doctor's nurse line or go to urgent care. For life-threatening symptoms, call 911.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: The terms governing your use of Health Made Clear.
terms.disclaimerBody: Health Made Clear is not a medical provider. The content on this platform is for educational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional with any questions you have about a medical condition.
acceptanceBody: By accessing or using Health Made Clear, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the platform.
We may update these terms at any time. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
serviceBody: Health Made Clear provides educational content about health topics. All content is for informational and educational purposes only.
We do not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. We are not a healthcare provider and do not replace the relationship between you and your healthcare providers.
The platform is offered free of charge. We do not sell user data or display advertising.
accountsBody: You may create an account to track your learning progress. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials.
You must provide accurate information when creating an account. You may delete your account at any time through the settings page.
prohibitedBody: You agree not to use the platform for any unlawful purpose or in violation of these terms. Prohibited activities include: misrepresenting your identity, attempting to access another user's account, using the platform to distribute malware, and scraping or reproducing content without permission.
intellectualBody: All content on Health Made Clear — including lesson materials, articles, glossary terms, and design — is owned by or licensed to Health Made Clear.
You may view, download, and print content for personal, non-commercial educational use. You may not reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works without permission.
liabilityBody: Health Made Clear provides content on an 'as is' basis. We make no warranties regarding the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the information.
To the fullest extent permitted by law, Health Made Clear shall not be liable for any damages arising from your use of the platform.
Always seek the advice of a qualified healthcare professional with any questions regarding your health.
changesBody: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date.
Your continued use of the platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
contactBody: If you have questions about these terms, please contact us through our Contact page.
